name: Route 1
description: The first route in Alola, connecting Iki Town to Hau'oli Outskirts. Rich with tall grass and diverse Pokemon.
storyRelevance: Your adventure begins here after receiving your starter from Professor Kukui.
unlockCondition: Available from the start of the game

items.name: Potion
items.location: Near the grass patch before the Trainer's School

items.name: Antidote
items.location: Behind the fence near the berry tree

items.name: Poke Ball
items.location: Gifted by the Professor after the festival

items.name: Paralyze Heal
items.location: Near the ledge overlooking the sea

tips: Great early grinding spot. Pikipek and Caterpie are common and give decent EXP. Pichu is rare but worth catching for its evolution into Alolan Raichu later. Visit the Trainer's School for Magnemite encounters.

name: Route 2
description: A route on Melemele Island connecting Hau'oli City to Verdant Cavern. Home to the Pokemon Center and a variety of encounters.
storyRelevance: You pass through here on the way to your first trial at Verdant Cavern. Team Skull grunts appear here.
unlockCondition: After completing events in Hau'oli City

items.name: TM01 Work Up
items.location: From Ilima after meeting him on Route 2

items.name: Super Potion
items.location: In the tall grass near the Pokemon Center

items.name: Revive
items.location: Behind the motel

items.name: Great Ball
items.location: Near the berry tree on the upper path

tips: Growlithe is an excellent early Fire-type if you didn't choose Litten. Abra will flee immediately so use a Quick Ball on your first turn. The Pokemon Center here is the last stop before Verdant Cavern.

name: Route 3
description: A mountainous route on Melemele Island connecting Verdant Cavern to Melemele Meadow. Known for its flying Pokemon shadows and the extremely rare Bagon encounter.
storyRelevance: After completing the Verdant Cavern trial, you traverse Route 3 to reach Melemele Meadow and eventually Iki Town for the Grand Trial.
unlockCondition: After completing the Verdant Cavern trial

encounters.notes: Ultra Sun only, flying shadow encounter
Ultra Moon only, flying shadow encounter

items.name: Heal Ball
items.location: Along the main path

items.name: Sharp Beak
items.location: Near the bridge area

items.name: Super Potion
items.location: Off the main path near tall grass

items.name: Nest Ball
items.location: Near the entrance to Melemele Meadow

tips: Bagon has a 1% encounter rate here and is the earliest you can get a pseudo-legendary in the game. It evolves into Salamence at level 50. Hawlucha at 10% is also excellent for its Fighting/Flying dual typing. In Ultra Sun, Rufflet appears from flying shadows and evolves into Braviary at level 54.

name: Hau'oli Outskirts
description: The outskirts of Hau'oli City on Melemele Island. A coastal area connecting Route 1 to Hau'oli City proper, with grassy patches and ocean access.
storyRelevance: You pass through here early in the game on the way to Hau'oli City. Professor Kukui's lab is located on the beachfront here.
unlockCondition: After traveling through Route 1

items.name: Potion
items.location: Near Kukui's lab on the beach

items.name: Poke Ball x5
items.location: From Professor Kukui after events

tips: Slowpoke is a great early catch for a Water/Psychic type that can eventually evolve into Slowbro or Slowking. Professor Kukui's lab is here on the beachfront. Surfing encounters become available later after obtaining Lapras Paddle.

name: Ten Carat Hill
description: A cave and grassy hollow on Melemele Island accessible from Route 1. The Farthest Hollow area requires Tauros Charge to access and contains rare Pokemon like Rockruff.
storyRelevance: An optional exploration area on Melemele Island. The Farthest Hollow is one of the few early places to catch Rockruff, and Carbink/Sableye can be found via SOS battles in the cave.
unlockCondition: Cave accessible early; Farthest Hollow requires Tauros Charge (after Melemele Grand Trial)

encounters.notes: SOS call from Carbink in cave

items.name: Nugget
items.location: Deep in the cave area

items.name: Hard Stone
items.location: In the Farthest Hollow

items.name: Star Piece
items.location: Hidden in the cave near Carbink encounters

items.name: Burn Heal
items.location: Near the cave entrance

tips: Rockruff is a fan-favorite that evolves into Lycanroc. In Ultra Sun, it evolves into Midday Form (level 25 during day) or Dusk Form (level 25 at dusk, 5-6 PM). Mawile is a strong Steel/Fairy type worth catching. Sableye can only appear via SOS calls from Carbink. The Farthest Hollow requires Tauros Charge to break the rocks blocking entry.

name: Verdant Cavern
description: A cave on Melemele Island that serves as the location for the first Island Trial. Captain Ilima's trial takes place here against the Totem Pokemon.
storyRelevance: The site of your first Island Trial. Captain Ilima challenges you to defeat the Totem Gumshoos (Ultra Sun) or Totem Raticate (Ultra Moon).
unlockCondition: After receiving the trial amulet and speaking with Captain Ilima

items.name: TM31 Brick Break
items.location: Deep inside the cavern, past the trial area

items.name: TM46 Thief
items.location: In the back room after completing the trial

items.name: Normalium Z
items.location: Reward for defeating the Totem Pokemon

items.name: X Defense
items.location: Near the entrance on a ledge

items.name: Super Potion
items.location: Hidden in the corner near Totem's den

trainers.name: Totem Gumshoos
trainers.notes: Ultra Sun Totem Pokemon. Boosts all stats by +1. Can call Yungoos as ally.

trainers.name: Totem Raticate (Alolan)
trainers.notes: Ultra Moon Totem Pokemon. Boosts Speed by +1. Can call Rattata as ally.

tips: The Totem Gumshoos/Raticate can call for help. Focus on the Totem first. Fighting-type moves are super effective against both. Use Makuhita if you traded for one in the Pokemon Center. Bring Potions and status heals. After the trial, explore the back of the cave for TM31 Brick Break and TM46 Thief.

name: Hau'oli City
description: The largest city on Melemele Island, featuring a shopping district, beachfront, marina, and the Trainer's School. The first major city you visit.
storyRelevance: You explore here after Route 1. Team Skull makes their first appearance. Captain Ilima can be battled near the marina.
unlockCondition: After passing through Route 1 and Hau'oli Outskirts

items.name: TM10 Hidden Power
items.location: From an NPC inside the mall

items.name: Silk Scarf
items.location: Ilima's house, from his father

items.name: X Attack
items.location: Near the police station

items.name: Poke Ball x10
items.location: Gift from Hau after exploring

tips: Explore thoroughly before heading to Route 2. The shopping district has useful items. Visit Ilima's house for a free Silk Scarf that powers up Normal-type moves. Magnemite in the Trainer's School area is an excellent early Steel-type.

name: Route 4
description: The first route on Akala Island, connecting Heahea City to Paniola Town. Features open grassy fields and a gentle slope.
storyRelevance: Your first exploration on Akala Island. Hau accompanies you as you head toward Paniola Ranch and the next trials.
unlockCondition: After arriving on Akala Island via ferry from Melemele

items.name: Adrenaline Orb
items.location: On the path near the tall grass

items.name: Energy Root
items.location: Off the main path to the left

items.name: Poison Barb
items.location: Near the fence by Paniola entrance

items.name: Great Ball
items.location: Along the path from Heahea

tips: Eevee is rare (5%) but very worth hunting for, as it has 8 possible evolutions. Mudbray is an excellent physical attacker for your team. Check berry trees for Crabrawler encounters.

name: Paniola Ranch
description: A large ranch on Akala Island home to the Pokemon Nursery (breeding). Features open fields with Mudbray, Mareep, and rare Tauros and Miltank encounters.
storyRelevance: You visit the ranch on your way to Route 5 and Brooklet Hill. The Pokemon Nursery here allows breeding for the first time. Hau gives you a tour.
unlockCondition: After passing through Paniola Town from Route 4

items.name: Amulet Coin
items.location: From a man inside the ranch house

items.name: Moomoo Milk
items.location: From the woman near the Miltank pen

items.name: Repeat Ball
items.location: Near the nursery

tips: Grab the Amulet Coin from the ranch house immediately. It doubles money from trainer battles and is essential for funding your adventure. Mareep is a great Electric-type that evolves into Ampharos. The Pokemon Nursery allows breeding. Leave two compatible Pokemon and walk around to hatch eggs.

name: Route 5
description: A route on Akala Island between Paniola Ranch and Brooklet Hill. Features a Pokemon Center and leads to the second trial.
storyRelevance: Team Skull grunts block the path initially. Gladion battles you here for the first time with his Type: Null.
unlockCondition: After events at Paniola Ranch

items.name: TM41 Torment
items.location: On a ledge accessible from above

items.name: Hyper Potion
items.location: Near the Pokemon Center

items.name: Super Repel
items.location: Past the bridge to the south

tips: Gladion's Type: Null is tough at this point. Make sure your team is at least level 17-18. Fomantis is good for a Grass-type if you need one. The Pokemon Center is the last stop before Brooklet Hill.

name: Route 6
description: A route on Akala Island connecting Heahea City to Royal Avenue. Features grassy fields with Oricorio and a rare Eevee encounter.
storyRelevance: You pass through here on the way to Royal Avenue and the Battle Royal Dome. Hapu and her Mudsdale make an appearance.
unlockCondition: After arriving on Akala Island

encounters.notes: SOS call from Eevee during day
SOS call from Eevee during night

items.name: TM88 Sleep Talk
items.location: Along the eastern path

items.name: X Sp. Atk
items.location: In the grass area near the fence

items.name: Elixir
items.location: Near the south exit to Royal Avenue

tips: Another spot to hunt for Eevee at 5% encounter rate. Even better, Eevee can SOS call Espeon (day) or Umbreon (night) which saves you evolution effort. Oricorio's Pa'u Style is a decent Psychic/Flying type. The route connects to Royal Avenue where you can try the Battle Royal.

name: Route 7
description: A coastal surfing and fishing route on Akala Island connecting Wela Volcano Park to Royal Avenue. Primarily a water route with no walking grass encounters.
storyRelevance: You pass through this water route between Wela Volcano Park and Royal Avenue. It serves as a transition between the fire and water areas of Akala.
unlockCondition: After completing Lana's trial at Brooklet Hill (for Lapras Paddle surfing)

items.name: Dive Ball
items.location: On the small beach area

items.name: Pearl
items.location: Hidden in the rocks near the shore

tips: Staryu is rare at 5% via fishing but evolves into the excellent Starmie with a Water Stone. Starmie can also appear via SOS from Staryu. This is a great grinding spot for Water-type encounters. Wishiwashi at level 20+ transforms into School Form in battle, making it incredibly powerful.

name: Royal Avenue
description: A festive avenue on Akala Island centered around the Battle Royal Dome. Features shops, the Thrifty Megamart, and the Battle Royal where four trainers fight simultaneously.
storyRelevance: You are introduced to the Battle Royal here, a four-way battle format. The Masked Royal (Professor Kukui) appears. Team Skull grunts cause trouble at the Thrifty Megamart.
unlockCondition: After traveling through Route 6 or Route 7

items.name: TM44 Rest
items.location: From an NPC inside the Battle Royal Dome

items.name: Effort Wing Set
items.location: Reward from winning Battle Royals

items.name: Flame Orb
items.location: Battle Royal prize exchange (48 BP)

tips: The Battle Royal Dome lets you exchange BP (Battle Points) for rare items and TMs. Stock up on healing items at the Thrifty Megamart here for cheaper prices. The Masked Royal is obviously Professor Kukui, but the game plays it straight.

name: Route 9
description: A water route on Akala Island accessible by Lapras Paddle from Konikoni City. Known for fishing Corsola and the rare Mareanie SOS encounter.
storyRelevance: You explore this route while investigating the Diglett Tunnel and heading to Memorial Hill. A police officer tasks you with defeating some Team Skull grunts here.
unlockCondition: After obtaining Lapras Paddle from Brooklet Hill trial

encounters.notes: SOS call from Corsola only. Corsola calls Mareanie as its predator.
Interactable overworld encounter near the police station

items.name: Net Ball
items.location: On the shore near the police station

items.name: Pearl String
items.location: Hidden in the rocks along the waterway

items.name: TM29 Psychic
items.location: Reward from the police officer after defeating Team Skull

tips: Mareanie is one of the most sought-after Pokemon here. To find it, fish for Corsola and let Corsola call for help via SOS. Mareanie has a low chance of appearing as a Corsola SOS ally since Mareanie preys on Corsola. Be patient. Ditto near the police station is essential for breeding.

name: Brooklet Hill
description: A lush waterfall area on Akala Island where Captain Lana's Water-type trial takes place. The trial involves investigating splashing in various pools.
storyRelevance: Captain Lana's trial site. You must investigate mysterious splashing in the water, eventually facing the Totem Araquanid.
unlockCondition: After completing events on Route 5

encounters.notes: Extremely rare via fishing and SOS

items.name: Waterium Z
items.location: Reward for completing Captain Lana's trial

items.name: Net Ball
items.location: Near the first pool area

items.name: Hyper Potion
items.location: Hidden near the waterfall

items.name: TM55 Scald
items.location: After the trial, near the exit

trainers.name: Totem Araquanid
trainers.notes: Totem Pokemon. +1 Speed. Calls Masquerain or Dewpider as ally. Uses Water Bubble ability to boost Water moves.

tips: The Totem Araquanid is bulky with Water Bubble boosting its Water-type attacks. Electric and Grass types are your best bet. Bring a Pikachu or Fomantis. The ally Dewpider can use Infestation to trap you, so try to KO it quickly. Wishiwashi's School Form in the trial pools is an appetizer for the real challenge.

name: Wela Volcano Park
description: A volcanic area on Akala Island where Captain Kiawe's Fire-type trial takes place. You must spot differences in dance performances.
storyRelevance: Captain Kiawe's trial involves watching Marowak dance performances and identifying the odd one out, culminating in a battle against Totem Alolan Marowak.
unlockCondition: After completing Lana's trial at Brooklet Hill

encounters.notes: Only females evolve into Salazzle (12.5% chance female)

items.name: Firium Z
items.location: Reward for completing Captain Kiawe's trial

items.name: Charcoal
items.location: Near the entrance to the volcano

items.name: TM39 Rock Tomb
items.location: Along the mountainside path

items.name: Burn Heal
items.location: Near a ledge on the volcanic slopes

trainers.name: Totem Marowak (Alolan)
trainers.notes: Totem Pokemon. +1 Speed boost. Fire/Ghost type. Calls Salazzle as ally. Uses Shadow Bone and Flame Wheel.

tips: Totem Alolan Marowak is Fire/Ghost and calls Salazzle as an ally. Water types dominate here. Rock and Dark types also work well. Watch out for the Salazzle ally's Poison Gas. Cubone evolves into Alolan Marowak when leveled up at night starting at level 28. Female Salandit (12.5% female rate) are needed to get Salazzle.

name: Lush Jungle
description: A dense jungle on Akala Island hosting Captain Mallow's Grass-type trial. You must gather ingredients to lure out the Totem Pokemon.
storyRelevance: Captain Mallow's trial requires collecting Mago Berry, Honey, and Big Root to cook a special meal that attracts the Totem Lurantis.
unlockCondition: After completing Kiawe's trial at Wela Volcano Park

items.name: Grassium Z
items.location: Reward for completing Captain Mallow's trial

items.name: Miracle Seed
items.location: Deep in the jungle near the Moss Rock

items.name: TM86 Grass Knot
items.location: In the western section of the jungle

items.name: Big Root
items.location: Trial ingredient - found in the jungle

items.name: Mago Berry
items.location: Trial ingredient - from a berry tree

items.name: Honey
items.location: Trial ingredient - from a Fomantis area

trainers.name: Totem Lurantis
trainers.notes: Totem Pokemon. +1 Speed boost. Uses Solar Blade with Power Herb for instant use. Calls Kecleon or Comfey as ally.

tips: Totem Lurantis is one of the hardest early trials. It uses Power Herb + Solar Blade for a devastating first-turn nuke. Its Kecleon ally can use Camouflage, and Comfey can heal with Floral Healing. Fire and Flying types are the best counters. The Moss Rock is here for evolving Eevee into Leafeon. Comfey is a strong Fairy-type healer if you can find one.

name: Route 8
description: A coastal route on Akala Island connecting Lush Jungle to Route 5. Features diverse terrain including rocky areas near the shore.
storyRelevance: After completing the Lush Jungle trial, you traverse Route 8 heading toward the Dimensional Research Lab and further Akala exploration.
unlockCondition: After completing Mallow's trial at Lush Jungle

items.name: TM58 Sky Drop
items.location: Near the Fossil Restoration Center

items.name: Water Stone
items.location: In the tide pool area (hidden)

items.name: Zygarde Cell
items.location: Near the route sign

items.name: Rare Candy
items.location: Hidden behind rocks near the shore

tips: Stufful is surprisingly strong with Fluffy ability and evolves into the powerful Bewear at level 27. The Fossil Restoration Center on this route lets you revive fossils. Salandit is worth catching but remember only females evolve into Salazzle.

name: Route 10
description: A mountainous route on Ula'ula Island leading up to the Hokulani Observatory and Mount Hokulani. Features a bus stop and tall grass with strong Pokemon.
storyRelevance: You travel through Route 10 on the way to Captain Sophocles' Electric-type trial at the Hokulani Observatory. The bus takes you up Mount Hokulani.
unlockCondition: After arriving on Ula'ula Island and exploring Malie City

items.name: TM74 Gyro Ball
items.location: Near the bus stop

items.name: Thunder Stone
items.location: Hidden near the tall grass

items.name: Max Repel
items.location: Along the mountain trail

tips: Skarmory is an excellent Steel/Flying defensive Pokemon at 10% encounter rate. Pancham evolves into Pangoro at level 32 if you have a Dark-type in your party. The bus at the top of Route 10 takes you to Hokulani Observatory for Sophocles' trial.

name: Route 11
description: A route on Ula'ula Island connecting Route 10 to Route 12. Known for the unique Komala encounters, a Normal-type that is always asleep.
storyRelevance: You traverse Route 11 heading toward the Secluded Shore and Route 12 as you continue exploring Ula'ula Island.
unlockCondition: After completing Sophocles' trial at Hokulani Observatory

items.name: TM74 Gyro Ball
items.location: Along the route path

items.name: Rare Candy
items.location: Hidden near the cliff edge

items.name: Zygarde Cell
items.location: Near the route sign

tips: Komala is unique - its Comatose ability means it is always considered asleep, making it immune to other status conditions. It has decent attack stats. Shiinotic at night is a rare Grass/Fairy type with useful support moves.

name: Route 12
description: A rocky mountain trail on Ula'ula Island that requires Mudsdale Gallop to traverse the rough terrain. Features Graveler, Torkoal, and the rare Manectric.
storyRelevance: You navigate this rocky route using Mudsdale to reach Secluded Shore and continue toward Route 13. The terrain is impassable without the Mudsdale ride.
unlockCondition: Requires Mudsdale Gallop ride Pokemon

items.name: Max Potion
items.location: Along the rocky trail

items.name: Nugget
items.location: Hidden in the rocks off the main path

items.name: TM77 Psych Up
items.location: Near the cliff edge

tips: Torkoal is a solid Fire-type wall with high Defense and access to Drought (hidden ability via SOS). Manectric is a strong Electric-type attacker. Mudsdale can appear as an SOS ally from Mudbray, saving you the effort of evolving one. Use Mudsdale ride to cross the rocky terrain.

name: Route 13
description: A desert-adjacent route on Ula'ula Island connecting Route 12 to Tapu Village. The route itself is barren, with encounters limited to fishing only.
storyRelevance: You pass through this desolate route on the way to Tapu Village and the Aether House. Haina Desert is accessible from here.
unlockCondition: After traversing Route 12

items.name: Max Repel
items.location: Along the sandy path

items.name: Comet Shard
items.location: Hidden near the oasis

items.name: Zygarde Cell
items.location: Near the route entrance

tips: Bruxish is rare at 5% via fishing but is a solid Water/Psychic type with Strong Jaw ability boosting biting moves. The oasis fishing spot has better rates for rare Pokemon. Haina Desert is accessible from this route but is easy to get lost in.

name: Haina Desert
description: A confusing desert maze on Ula'ula Island. Harsh sunlight blazes during the day while sandstorms rage at night. Navigation requires following specific rock cairn patterns to avoid going in circles.
storyRelevance: An optional exploration area accessible from Route 13. You can find the Psychium Z here at the altar, and it connects to the Ruins of Abundance where Tapu Bulu resides.
unlockCondition: Accessible from Route 13 after reaching Tapu Village

items.name: Psychium Z
items.location: At the altar deep in the desert (follow the rock cairns)

items.name: TM85 Dream Eater
items.location: In the northern section of the desert

items.name: Soft Sand
items.location: Near the entrance

items.name: Rare Candy
items.location: Hidden among the sand dunes

tips: Navigation is tricky. To reach the altar with Psychium Z, follow the pattern of rock cairns (stacked stones). Gabite is a rare SOS encounter during sandstorm and eventually evolves into Garchomp. Trapinch in rustling sand spots evolves into Vibrava (level 35) and then Flygon (level 45). Krokorok evolves into Krookodile at level 40.

name: Mount Lanakila
description: The tallest mountain in Alola, located on Ula'ula Island. Its snow-capped peak hosts the newly built Pokemon League. Features ice cave sections with high-level Pokemon.
storyRelevance: The final area before the Pokemon League. You must climb through the mountain's caves and exterior to reach the summit. Gladion battles you here before the League.
unlockCondition: After completing all Grand Trials and the Vast Poni Canyon trial

items.name: TM13 Ice Beam
items.location: In the ice cave section

items.name: Full Restore
items.location: Near the mountainside path

items.name: Max Revive
items.location: Hidden in the cave behind ice formations

items.name: Icium Z
items.location: In the cave near the summit

items.name: Rare Candy
items.location: On a ledge overlooking the mountain

trainers.name: Gladion
trainers.notes: Silvally's type matches your starter weakness

tips: Stock up on Full Restores, Revives, and status heals before ascending. Gladion's Silvally holds a memory disc matching your starter's weakness. Sneasel evolves into Weavile with a Razor Claw at night. Alolan Vulpix (Ultra Sun) evolves into Ninetales with an Ice Stone. The Pokemon League awaits at the summit.

name: Vast Poni Canyon
description: A sprawling canyon on Poni Island that serves as the site of the Dragon-type trial. Features multiple cave areas and outdoor sections with high-level Pokemon including the Jangmo-o evolution line.
storyRelevance: The site of the Dragon-type trial, one of the last trials before the Pokemon League. The Totem Kommo-o awaits at the end. Lillie accompanies you through parts of the canyon.
unlockCondition: After completing Hapu's Grand Trial on Exeggutor Island

encounters.notes: Top outdoor areas, Midday Form (day) or Midnight Form (night)
SOS call from Carbink in cave areas

items.name: Dragonium Z
items.location: Reward for defeating Totem Kommo-o

items.name: TM02 Dragon Claw
items.location: Deep in the canyon cave

items.name: Full Restore
items.location: Near the trial area entrance

items.name: Max Revive
items.location: Hidden in the cave behind a boulder

items.name: Rare Candy
items.location: On the outdoor cliffside path

trainers.name: Totem Kommo-o
trainers.notes: Totem Pokemon. All stats +1. Calls Scizor (Lv 46) and Noivern (Lv 48) as allies. Dragon/Fighting type.

tips: Totem Kommo-o is extremely dangerous with +1 to all stats and powerful allies Scizor and Noivern. Fairy types are ideal since Kommo-o is 4x weak to Fairy. Bring a strong Fairy like Ribombee, Mimikyu, or Gardevoir. Jangmo-o at 5% in the top areas is your chance to catch the pseudo-legendary Dragon line. Lillie heals your team at certain points in the canyon.

name: Poni Plains
description: A wide-open grassy area on Poni Island with some of the highest-level wild Pokemon in the game. Features rare encounters like Tauros, Miltank, and version-exclusive Cottonee/Petilil.
storyRelevance: A post-game exploration area on Poni Island unlocked after becoming Champion. Features extremely high-level wild Pokemon for training and catching.
unlockCondition: After becoming Champion and completing the main story

encounters.notes: Shaking plant spots, great for EXP grinding

items.name: Max Elixir
items.location: In the tall grass area

items.name: PP Max
items.location: Near the Poni Meadow entrance

items.name: Rare Candy
items.location: Hidden among the flowers

items.name: TM24 Thunderbolt
items.location: Along the cliff path

tips: This is the best post-game grinding spot. Chansey from shaking plants gives massive EXP. Use an Adrenaline Orb and False Swipe to trigger SOS chains for shiny hunting or EV training. Scyther from shaking plants can be evolved into Scizor via Metal Coat trade. Wild Pokemon here are level 54-57, so bring a strong team.
